title: Anti-ICE Quebradita and Letter Writing to ICE detainees
description:
Anti-ICE Quebradita and Letter Writing to ICE detainees, with Irvin Gonzalez and Dignity Not Detention    
  Sunday, April 26th | 4:30 PM – 6:30 PM   
  1639 18th Street. Santa Monica, CA 90404   
  Free + Open to the Public, Registration Required   
  Quebradita is a Mexican and Mexican American social dance form that first emerged in the 1990s and continues to be practiced today. Rooted in transborder belonging, working-class creativity, and resistance, quebradita has enabled minoritized communities to cultivate and sustain “alegría Mexicana” amid increasingly neoliberal conditions that displace and relocate working-class Mexican and Mexican American families. title: Dzogchen-LA meditation
description:
Dzogchen-LA meditation   
  Tuesday, April 28th | 7 PM -8:30 PM   
  1639 18th Street. Santa Monica, CA 90404   
  Free + Open to the Public   
   Join us for a healing series of every-other Tuesday evening guided meditations,    
  where we will learn to open and become Aware of our own innermost Heart-Essence Awareness.   
   Emerging from the Dzogchen lineage of Tibetan Buddhism. This is not just a Tuesday evening “mind practice,” but a doorway into   living from and within freedom  —freedom not only from our own psychology and historic self-narrative, but also from the habitual creative loops that keep us recycling the same expressions. Over time, we learn to flow through life with the lived experience of non-duality within duality—and duality within non-duality.

title: LOCAL OCCURRENCE
description:
Tickets  



   LOCAL OCCURRENCE  , is a project that I, Henryk Golden, am starting together with the violinist Myra Hinrichs and the percussionist Corey Fogel, performing written and improvised music. The goal is to work with a set group of artists based in Southern California, including the trio itself, Ana Luisa Díaz de Cossío and Daniel Allas and experiment with what is possible with our trio over a series of OCCURRENCES. We use our instruments as tools to shape the sound and space the audience experiences. This includes the use of amplification, distortionation and the physical dislocation within the space. 



 We hope to be in dialogue with the audience and between ourselves on what directions we can take. Creating a music community that is local to LA, especially in a social sense.

title: Dafne Vicente-Sandoval and Charles Curtis
description:
Bassoonist  Dafne Vicente-Sandoval  and cellist  Charles Curtis  perform live-in-concert – at an event pulled by UPEND – premiering a new version of  Tashi Wada ’s 2017  Witness , originally written for Vicente-Sandoval on bassoon and now including accompaniment by Curtis on cello. In Witness, a series of microtonally inflected scales and tetrachords are explored through repetition, variation, and improvisation. At this intimate concert, Curtis will also present brief solo cello pieces, possibly including music by Tashi Wada, Morton Feldman, Christian Wolff, Alvin Lucier, and/or other works in progress. Vicente-Sandoval’s own  Minos Circuit Rewired , for microphone feedback, voice, and disassembled bassoon, will close the evening.

Songs to Quell the Monster Program 1 of 4: A Mother’s Lullaby
description:
Saturday, May 2nd. Doors open at 6PM, Starts at 6:30PM. 



 Pay What You Can (Suggested $10) 



  TICKETS  



 Growing up, we are first held by the voice of our mothers—its rhythms teaching us how to listen, how to imagine, how to feel our way through the world before we fully understand it. A lullaby can soothe, but it can also shield, shaping what is heard and what remains unsaid. In this opening program,  A Mother’s Lullaby , Milisuthando Bongela’s  Milisuthando  returns to the fragile architectures of such protection, tracing a childhood in the Transkei where the violences of apartheid were both ever-present and eerily obscured. Tuesday, May 5th | 5PM – 7PM 
18th Street Arts Center 
1639 18th Street. Santa Monica, CA 90404

  These gatherings are dedicated to gathering our community around good food, conversation, and art. Please stop by for a bite and chat with our community and spotlight artist  Deirdre Mulrooney.   
  Artist bio    
   Artist portrait by Neil Hoare.  
  Dee Mulrooney is a multidisciplinary artist whose work spans painting, drawing, film, storytelling, and performance. Rooted in her working-class Irish experience, Mulrooney’s practice explores identity, class, exile, displacement, and the realities of inhabiting a female body. description:
A Healing Story Circle: A Return to the Roots with Miguel Rivera and Michael Rey 
Wednesday, May 13th l 5PM – 7PM 
18th Street Arts Center 
1639 18th Street. Santa Monica, CA 90404  
 Free + Open to the Public   
  As modern culture and civilization have evolved in the past decades, we have continually removed ourselves from a consistent relation to nature and its cycles. No longer are we familiar with the gestation cycles of the plants and animals that surround us. This creates an alienation from the world around us, a separation from nature, a virtual reality.  
  This event is an invitation to create paths to the world around us, to renew the relationship and sustain it so we may continue to be connected. To explore old ways that have resurfaced again so we may benefit from them i

title: Collective Choreographies
description: Collective Choreographies showcases thirty-six labanotational ink drawings that depict line dances. Using a taxonomy of tiny and precise lines, the notation system isolates individual movements. Each notational type corresponds to a single movement type, as specified by “stepsheets,” which are the written instructions popularly circulated through open-source websites for teaching and learning line dances. Each drawing shows 121 “dancers” in a grid of 11 x 11 moving simultaneously. These hand-drawn depictions highlight the inherent tension between coordinated, identical movements and unique, idiosyncratic instances. title: FROM JA to SHOHEI: Baseball and the Japanese American Experience
description:
FROM JA to Shohei: Baseball and the Japanese American Experience 
Saturday, May 16th l 11AM – 3PM 
 Video Screening & Panel Discussion    
   18th Street Arts Center 
1639 18th Street. Santa Monica, CA 90404  
 Free + Open to the Public   
  Stripped of their constitutional rights, their homes, businesses, and dignity, 120,000 Japanese Americans were forced into dusty desolate camps, surrounded by armed guards and barbed wire.  
  Ironically it was the All-American pastime of baseball that saved their sanity.  
    Baseball Behind Barbed Wire    
   Screening of    Baseball Behind Barbed Wire,    Yuriko Gamo Romer  ‘s outstanding documentary short, plus a lively panel discussion with author, historian, professor   Susan H. Kamei;   author, filmmaker, Nisei Baseball Research

title: Dzogchen-LA meditation
description:
Dzogchen-LA meditation   
  Tuesday, May 26th | 7 PM -8:30 PM   
  1639 18th Street. Santa Monica, CA 90404   
  Free + Open to the Public   
   Join us for a healing series of every-other Tuesday evening guided meditations,    
  where we will learn to open and become Aware of our own innermost Heart-Essence Awareness.   
   Emerging from the Dzogchen lineage of Tibetan Buddhism. This is not just a Tuesday evening “mind practice,” but a doorway into   living from and within freedom  —freedom not only from our own psychology and historic self-narrative, but also from the habitual creative loops that keep us recycling the same expressions. Over time, we learn to flow through life with the lived experience of non-duality within duality—and duality within non-duality.   
   From an ever-deepe

title: Shorter Than a Tunnel, Longer Than a Bridge
description:
Shorter Than a Tunnel, Longer Than a Bridge  Amelia Charter and Sam Wentz Friday May 29 and Saturday May 30, 7pm  Sunday May 31st, 4pm   Amelia Charter and Sam Wentz collaborate for the first time in an evening-length performance that gathers a growing body of materials: things are always tied to us – dragging, following. Working at the intersection of performance, object practice, and poetry, Charter and Wentz construct an elemental system of maintenance that tends toward disruption. Stalking the ethics of care, not expression. Intelligence moves without authority. It works knowing it may feel.

  Tuesday, June 9th | 7 PM -8:30 PM   
  1639 18th Street. Santa Monica, CA 90404   
  Free + Open to the Public   
   Join us for a healing series of every-other Tuesday evening guided meditations,    
  where we will learn to open and become Aware of our own innermost Heart-Essence Awareness.   
   Emerging from the Dzogchen lineage of Tibetan Buddhism. This is not just a Tuesday evening “mind practice,” but a doorway into   living from and within freedom  —freedom not only from our own psychology and historic self-narrative, but also from the habitual creative loops that keep us recycling the same expressions. Over time, we learn to flow through life with the lived experience of non-duality within duality—and duality within non-duality.   
   From an ever-deepe

title: Beck + Col: Red Night Slumber Party Screening
description:
Beck + Col: Red Night    Slumber Party Screening  
Saturday, June 13th l 7PM – 9PM 
    18th Street Arts Center 
1639 18th Street. Santa Monica, CA 90404  
 Free + Open to the Public   
  Enter the colorful world of a queer chosen family of five monsters.  
    Red Night    highlights the helplessness of individualism through the narrative of a slasher film. In typical slasher fashion, victims are pursued and killed when they are on their own, but when the characters are together they are able to counter the seemingly indestructible killer.

description:
HIDING IN PLAIN SIGHT: Manzanar’s Camouflage Net Factory    
  Multimedia Presentation & Participatory Camo Net Weaving Workshop   
  Saturday, July 18, 2026   
  11:00 AM – 3:00 PM   
   1639 18th Street. Santa Monica, CA 90404   
  Free + Open to the Public, Registration required for Part 2   
  11AM Part 1 – A multimedia presentation on the short-lived and controversial camouflage net factory created in Manzanar in 1942. Featuring archival photos by distinguished photographer Dorothea Lange.  
  12PM Part 2 (Reservation required) – A fun, hands-on workshop for registered participants to weave on a free-style interpretation of a camouflage net!
